rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 4] The December 28, 2023 Order Continuing Hearing on Petition for Approve Compromise of Pending Action and Requiring Further Documentation, par. 5, 6, requested further information as to each Doe plaintiff regarding whether any medical liens, or amounts claimed by Medicare or Medi-Cal, were claimed or due at the time of the pending settlement agreement. The February 23, 2024 Amended Petition to Approve Compromise of Pending Action, at paragraph 13 states, "Claimant's medical expenses, including medical expenses paid by petitioner and insurers, to be reimbursed from proceeds of settlement or judgment." Subsections 13(b)(3) and (b)(4) are checked. Paragraph 15(a) is checked, stating "Petitioner has paid none of the claimant's expenses listed in items 13 and 14 for which reimbursement is requested." Paragraph 19(a)(1) is checked and states, "Disposition of balance of proceeds of settlement or judgment/Petitioner requests that the balance of the proceeds of the settlement or judgment be disbursed as follows: / There is a guardianship of the estate of the minor or a conservatorship of the estate of the adult person with a disability filed in San Francisco Superior Court case number see attachment 19a(1)./ see attachment 19a(1) of the proceeds in money or other property will be paid or delivered to the guardian of the state of the minor or of the conservator of the estate of the conservatee. The money or other property is specified in Attachment 19a(1)." In the sealed Amended Attachments to Petition to Approve Compromise of Pending Action, filed February 23, 2024, there is an Attachment 13b(3), which states in relevant part "Counsel at Cotchett Pitre & McCarthy received confirmation over the phone that Jane Doe 1 has no liens. The firm is waiting to receive a written confirmation." There is no Attachment 19a(1) in this filing. The April 9, 2024 Order Approving Compromise of Claim or Action or Disposition of Proceeds of Judgment for Minor or Person with Disability. The April 2024 Full and Final Release discusses the existence or non-existence of liens which may attached to the settlement amount, which City and County of San Francisco may hold against the plaintiffs, and assumption of liability for future claims from Centers for Medicare & Medicaid arising out of the civil action. As was pointed out in the Petition for Instructions, this language appears to contradict the 2023 Settlement language at paragraph 4a. The April 2024 Full and Final Release was not before the court when it considered the February 23, 2024 Amended Petition to Approve Compromise of Pending Action or when it entered the April 2024 Order Approving Compromise of Claim. How should this court reconcile these documents? If the effect of the 2023 Settlement and the 2024 Full and Final Release is that the estate owes DHCS reimbursement for services, the court is also interested in whether the amount of the DHCS claim is correct. Here, DHCS presents a claim in the amount of $1,543,896.17, apparently founded on the costs of Ms. Dreste's lodging for the period of January 1, 2016 through January 31, 2023 at Laguna Honda Hospital and her use of the San Francisco Health Plan from February 1, 2023 through July 1, 2024 (per Pet. for Instr. at 3:20-23).

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 4 of 4] On the face of the relevant portions of Welf. & Inst. Code section 14009.5 and 42 U.S.C. section 1396p(b)(1)(B)(i), only "medical assistance correctly paid" on behalf of the individual" is recoverable from the individual's estate under the Medicaid estate recovery provision in 42 U.S.C. section 1396p(b)(1). Both the federal and state framework presupposes that services subject to the estate recovery provision were actually delivered or rendered to the individual. If the medical assistance was not "correctly paid," as that phrase is used on the face of the statute, the cost would arguably not be available for recovery from Ms. Dreste's estate under Welf. & Inst. Code section 140095 and 42 U.S.C. section 1396p. Is any part of the DHCS claim for services, including lodging, or medical assistance not "correctly paid" on behalf of Ms. Dreste, because they were not actually rendered during the period of alleged abuse between 2016 and January 2019, or if rendered, were alleged to consist of systemic abuse and privacy violations of Ms. Dreste over the course of several years?

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 4] The reason there is a question before this court as to whether the Medicaid estate recovery provision in 42 U.S.C. section 1396p(b)(1) and the companion state law are applicable in the Estate of Madeline Dreste, is that Ms. Dreste, as Jane Doe 1, was awarded $1,050,000 as her part of the 2023 settlement of civil litigation brought on behalf of certain patients of Laguna Honda Hospital and Rehabilitation Center. The civil litigation alleged hospital staff engaged in systemic abuse and privacy violations of the plaintiff patients over the course of several years, and at least between 2016 and January 2019. (See, San Francisco Superior Court Case No. CGC-21-592296. First Amended Complaint for Injunctive Relief and Damages, filed October 15, 2021, at paragraphs 2-6, 12, 18, 19, 28-31, 33-34, 35-38, 55-106.) Defendants filed an answer denying the allegations and asserting several affirmative defenses on January 29, 2021. Settlement of the litigation was executed on or about April 28, 2023. A copy of Stipulation Re Settlement is Exhibit A to SFPA's Petition for Instructions. Notice of Settlement Of Entire Case was filed in the civil action May 23, 2023. Judge Wiss approved the Settlement, and issued Order Approving Compromise of Claim or Disposition of Proceeds of Judgment for Minor of Person With Disability as to Jane Doe 1, Ms. Dreste, on April 9, 2024. Ms. Dreste died July 10, 2024. Her portion of the settlement, minus approved attorney's fees and costs, was released to her conservator, the SF Public Guardian two weeks later. Prior to distribution of the settlement proceeds, Ms. Dreste was indigent. As of the initiation of Conservatorship of Person and Estate of Ms. Dreste in 2016, her estate totaled approximately $2,500. (See, San Francisco Superior Court Case No. PCN-16-299678, Final Inventory and Appraisal filed January 30, 2017.) Her stay at Laguna Honda Hospital from 2016 until her death was paid for through Medi-Cal. The court does note paragraph 11 of the Stipulation Re Settlement document states "This is a 'no fault' settlement and neither Plaintiffs nor defendant nor San Francisco admit any liability or wrongdoing." Nevertheless, in the face of (1) the State of California Department of Public Health 2019 Statement of Deficiencies, referenced at paragraphs 28-29 and attached as Exhibit 1 of the amended civil complaint, (2) the allegations of abuse pleaded in the civil litigation - specifically as to Jane Doe 1 aka Ms. Dreste - and (3) the multi-million dollar settlement thereof reached on behalf of Ms. Dreste and others, the court is left to wonder whether Ms. Dreste actually received the services Laguna Honda Hospital billed to Medi-Cal between 2016 and 2019. If she did not actually receive those services, the DHCS claim amount against the estate should be reduced accordingly.

[Part 1 of 4] The court has reviewed the filings submitted in advance of the February 20, 2026 hearing. The hearing is continued to June 12, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., to allow for further briefing on the following issues, listed below. The parties shall each file and serve a brief addressing these issues by May 4, 2026. Parties may file and serve a response to any of these further briefs by May 15, 2026. Notice of the June 12, 2026 hearing and a copy of this tentative ruling will also be issued to Charles Jonas, as Guardian Ad Litem in the related civil action, Anne Marie Murphy, of Cotchette Pitre & McCarthy, LLP, and Kathryn Stebner and Deena Zacharin, of Stebner Gertler Guadagni & Kawamoto, as counsel for plaintiffs in the related civil action, and the San Francisco City Attorney's Office. The court anticipates each of them may have information or insight that would be helpful to the court in ruling on the pending Petition for Instructions, and they are asked to attend the hearing and invited to file briefs in this matter addressing any of the issues below. Issue # 1: What is the legal relationship and effect between the April 2023 Stipulation Re Settlement presented to Judge Wiss for approval in conjunction with the Petition for Approval of Compromise of Pending Action, and the April 2024 Full and Final Release between the parties, which was not presented to the court for approval and which did not exist when the April 9, 2024 Order Approving Compromise of Claim or Action or Disposition of Proceeds of Judgment for Minor or Person with Disability was entered? Issue # 1A: What liens or claims, if any, did City and County of San Francisco waive or release as part of settlement of the civil action? What liens or claims, if any, did plaintiffs accept liability for, as part of the settlement of the civil action? What knowledge, formally or otherwise, did City and County of San Francisco have at the time of the settlement of a potential future claim by Department of Health Care Services pursuant to Welf. & Inst. Code section 140095 and 42 U.S.C. section 1396p against Ms. Dreste's estate? Issue #2 Is any part of the Department of Health Care Services ("DHCS") claim against the estate for the costs of services or medical assistance which were not "correctly paid" under the language of the statute, because they were not actually rendered during the period of alleged abuse, between 2016 and January 2019? San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA")'s Petition for Instructions, filed April 15, 2025, touches upon this issue of the potential legal effect of the civil settlement in the related matter, San Francisco Superior Court Case No. CGC-21-592296, Jane Doe 1, et al vs. City and County of San Francisco, et al., on DHCS's pending reimbursement claims, beginning at page 6. The Petition also includes as attachments several documents from the civil matter: Stipulation re Settlement, executed on or about April 28, 2023; the April 9, 2024 Order Approving Compromise of Claim or Action or Disposition of Proceeds of Judgment for Minor or Person with Disability as to Jane Does 1, aka Ms. Dreste; Full and Final Release, executed on or around April 24, 2024. These documents, and other documents filed in the civil action, discuss or reference the existence, and/or waiver of medical liens or claims against the plaintiffs. To wit: The April 2023 Stipulation Re Settlement states at paragraph 4a, which is handwritten and apparently was added in open court, "San Francisco shall release any liens related to Plaintiff's medical care." And at paragraph 12, "Plaintiffs agree that as part of the settlement process they shall execute: (a) Centers for Medicare & Medicaid (CMS) waiver form so that the [sic] San Francisco can comply with its obligations under Medicare, Medicaid and SCHIP Extension Act of 2007; and (b) and IRS Form W-9."
